title: Issues 2060 2062 2064 lane remainder

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the remaining payload/dogfooding follow-ups from the just-merged #2058/#2059 lane as a stacked PR sequence: #2060 issue evidence reuse, #2064 bounded PR-comment repair closeout, and #2062 current-state payload upgrade attention planning.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not weaken planning closeout for broad lane completion, do not treat chat summaries as durable issue evidence, do not make start mutate the repo, do not overwrite host-owned payload surfaces, and keep long-hop upgrade work structured/recheckable rather than prose-only.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact stack boundaries, helper names, packet shapes, and focused test placement while preserving each issue's acceptance boundary.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement #2060 first, then #2064, then #2062 as stacked branches from current master.
proof_expectations: Focused CLI/runtime tests for each issue slice.
AW implement proof selection after changed paths are known.
Schema/reference/generated command checks when public packets or command surfaces change.
Workspace lint/typecheck and relevant broad workspace tests before PR publication.
completion_criteria: #2060 exposes a narrow structured issue-evidence refresh/import path so already-fetched issue data can satisfy external-intent grounding without trusting chat summaries.
#2064 lets bounded PR-comment repair work claim only PR-feedback-addressed progress without requiring active-plan closeout residue, while preserving broad lane/issue completion blockers.
#2062 adds a structured current-state payload upgrade attention plan for long-hop payload upgrades, with categories, affected surfaces, recheck command, and startup/doctor/report visibility.
The stack is published as PRs with semver labels and ready state after local and GitHub proof pass.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Issues 2060 2062 2064 lane remainder.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.outcome: Implement #2060, #2064, and #2062 as the remaining payload/dogfooding lane stack.
intent.constraints: Keep evidence durable and structured, keep closeout claims honest, and keep payload upgrade attention recheckable without hidden startup mutation.
intent.latitude: Choose bounded packet/helper/test shapes and stack order.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Start the #2060 branch from current master and implement the issue-evidence reuse slice.
execution.proof: Focused tests plus AW implement proof selection, then lint/typecheck/broad workspace checks before publishing.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Finish the remaining payload/dogfooding follow-ups after the #2058/#2059 merged stack.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Merged. Implement the remainder of the lane.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: After the #2058/#2059 stack merged, implement the still-open follow-ups created by that lane rather than reopening the already-closed #2046 parent.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Stack #2060, #2064, and #2062, with the long-hop payload attention plan last because it is the largest payload-contract change.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Workspace runtime/startup/implement/report helpers, config/schema/reference docs, generated command packages if command/public schema shape changes, focused CLI tests, and this planning record.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Expect 20-35 files across the full stack if schema/reference/generated surfaces change.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest per slice; AW implement --changed per slice; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; make typecheck; schema/reference/generated checks for public contract changes; runtime mirror consistency; git diff --check.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Preserve durable evidence boundaries, strict closeout for broad work, and host-owned payload safety.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact helper names, packet shape, tests, and stack details.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract
post_decomposition_delegation.route skipped reason: The stack touches shared runtime/config/schema behavior and needs local proof/rebase control across related PRs.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

immediate_next_action: Create branch agent/2060-issue-evidence-grounding from current master and implement #2060.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
validation_commands: uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py implement --changed <changed-paths> --task "Implement the remaining payload/dogfooding lane: #2060, #2064, #2062" --format json
uv run python scripts/run_agentic_workspace.py report --target . --section runtime_mirror_consistency --format json
completion_criteria: #2060, #2064, and #2062 are implemented in a stacked PR sequence.
Each PR has focused proof and semver labeling.
The final stack passes local proof and GitHub checks.
Any remaining residue is explicitly outside this lane.

execution_run.changed surfaces: none yet; execution has not changed files
execution_run.next step: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Create a bounded plan for Issues 2060 2062 2064 lane remainder.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
